REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT AND REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT IS IN MATERIAL BREACH OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. NO OPPOSITION FILED. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE OCTOBER 25, 2018 SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT STATES, "IF ANY PARTY TO THIS SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT COMMITS A MATERIAL BREACH OF THE TERMS OF THIS SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT (AS DETERMINED BY THE SAN FRANCISCO SUPERIOR COURT)[,] THEN THE PREVAILING PARTY [MAY] ENFORCE THE TERMS OF THIS SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT [AND RECOVER] THEIR REASONABLE ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS OF ENFORCING THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. THIS COURT HAS FOUND DEFENDANT TO HAVE COMMITTED A MATERIAL BREACH OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. ACCORDINGLY, REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES ARE GRANTED TO PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $13,005 (FEES INCURRED IN DIRECT CONNECTION WITH THE "ENFORCEMENT" OF THE AGREEMENT; ALL OTHER FEES AND COSTS IN THE AMOUNT OF $6,810 WERE NOT FOUND TO HAVE BEEN INCURRED IN DIRECT CONNECTION WITH THE "ENFORCEMENT" OF THE AGREEMENT). ALL DEFENDANTS SHALL WITHIN 30 DAYS SUBMIT ALL CURRENT DOCUMENTATION TO THE MORTGAGE BROKER, AND PROCURE THE LOAN WITHIN 60 DAYS. ATTORNEY'S FEES SHALL BE REMITTED NO LATER THAN 90 DAYS FROM ENTRY OF ORDER. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement And Request For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Jan-04-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES (via Zoom):

Andrew Serros; The Law Offices of Serros & Bauman; 415-710-8012; appearing on behalf of plaintiff Mary C. McFadden.
James F. McFadden; Law Offices of James F. McFadden; appearing on behalf of defendants James F. McFadden, Joseph P. McFadden, Leo J. McFadden, Patricia McFadden and Kathleen McFadden Vasquez.
J. Michael Phelps; 415-569-4108; appearing on behalf of defendants Mary L. McFadden, trustee, Margaret McFadden, Anne McFadden and Monica McFadden.

Mr. James F. McFadden contests the court's tentative ruling regarding the attorney's fees imposed on them, and states that defendants want to move forward with the settlement agreement and that they are "near the finish line." Defendant asks that the court grant additional time for the parties to tie up loose ends. Plaintiff does not object to defendants' request for additional time. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT AND REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part as follows: Plaintiff's Motion to Enforce Settlement is GRANTED. Defendant is in material breach of the settlement agreement. No opposition filed. Plaintiff's Request for Attorney's Fees is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The October 25, 2018 Settlement Agreement states, "If any party to this Settlement Agreement commits a material breach of the terms of this Settlement Agreement (as determined by the San Francisco Superior Court)[,] then the prevailing party [may] enforce the terms of this Settlement Agreement [and recover] their reasonable attorney fees and costs of enforcing the Settlement Agreement. This Court has found Defendant to have committed a material breach of the settlement agreement. Accordingly, reasonable attorney's fees are granted to Plaintiff in the amount of $13,005 (fees incurred in direct connection with the "enforcement" of the agreement; all other fees and costs in the amount of $6,810 were not found to have been incurred in direct connection with the "enforcement" of the agreement). All defendants shall within 30 days submit all current documentation to the mortgage broker, and procure the loan within 60 days. Attorney's fees shall be remitted no later than 90 days from entry of order.

Plaintiff's counsel to prepare order. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
